transcripts.title: IELTS Writing Task 2: Discussion essay (5)| Environmental problems | Cambridge 14

segments.text:
All right, let's write another discussion essay. 
This one is also from IELTS academic 14. As usual,

we need to understand the 
statement and instruction.

Some people say that the main environmental 
problem over time is the loss of particular

species of plants and animals. Others say that 
there are more important environmental problems.

So, it's about environmental problems and one 
specific one: the loss of species of plants and

animals, and if this is the main problem or issue 
of our time or not. We need to discuss both views,

meaning that we need to say what those 
people think and why they think like that,

and then give our opinion. So, we need 
to say they think the main problem is

the loss of plants and animals because, and 
also, there are more important environmental

problems. And then we can agree with V1 
or V2 or have a totally different view.

All right, it's time to decide. For view one, 
they think like that because all animals and

plants are necessary for our planet, and 
also they have the right to live, right?

And the second one, they're more important 
problems or issues, such as climate change and

deforestation. And now my view, I actually 
support view 2, and I agree with that.

Now it's time to write. The first thing to 
write is the paraphrase. Actually, for this one,

I've decided to add an introductory sentence. You 
don't have to; it's not a must. I just think it's

a good way to start this one. Today, environmental 
issues are a growing global concern. Among these

problems, the loss of specific species 
of plants and animals is debatable. So,

these two are my introductory sentences. Again, 
it's not a must, but I found it useful here.

And now it's time to paraphrase. Some people argue 
that it is the primary environmental problem or

error. While others contend that there are more 
pressing environmental challenges that deserve

attention. So here you can add your opinion if 
you like, but I've decided to state my opinion

in my conclusion. So, in my first paragraph, I 
will discuss SV1, and in the second one, I will

discuss VO2. In my conclusion, I'll summarize 
all the main points and will state my opinion.

For the first one, if you remember, I 
said all animals and plants are necessary,

and also they have the right to live. On 
the one hand, so I'll start with this one

because I want to discuss V1. The diversity 
of life forms on Earth is essential for the

planet's overall health, and every 
species' right to coexist on Earth

should be respected. This is actually a long 
topic sentence. Writing a more specific one,

I mean having only one point would be a better 
thing to do, but this is another way to do that.

Now it's time to explain the first one. When 
species disappear, it disrupts ecological

relationships. So what is the result of that? 
This, in turn, can have consequences for humans

because they depend on these ecosystems 
for resources such as food and clean water.

Now the second point, they have the right to 
live. Furthermore, I want to add another point,

so I use "furthermore." Each plant and 
animal deserves the chance to exist,

and it is not morally right to deprive 
them of that opportunity. Our moral duty

is to safeguard all species regardless 
of their immediate usefulness to us.

So here you could add examples to develop this 
paragraph further. This could be a good exercise

for you. You could copy and paste this one and 
add your details to improve this paragraph.

In the second one, I said climate change 
and deforestation are more important,

on the other hand, so I use this one to discuss 
the second view. Issues like climate change and

deforestation have more impacts on the 
environment. Why? I need to explain.

Climate change, for example, affects every 
corner of the globe and has consequences such

as rising sea levels, extreme weather 
events, and agricultural disruptions.

Now the second one, moreover, so I want to add 
the second one. I use "moreover." Deforestation,

as a grave and urgent issue, contributes 
to plant and animal loss and accelerates

climate change. So what is the result 
of that? These challenges have a more

direct and imminent impact on the planet's 
health and human societies, resulting in

immediate threats to our environment's 
food security and overall well-being.

Now it's time to write the conclusion. 
I start with "in conclusion," then I'll

add my opinion and summarize all 
the main points. In conclusion,

I believe there are more critical environmental 
issues than biodiversity loss. So this is my

view. I use biodiversity loss to paraphrase 
the loss of species of plants and animals.

Now it's time to summarize all the main points. 
It has undeniably significant, but other paramount

concerns like climate change and deforestation 
demand imperative attention and action. These

issues have far-reaching and immediate direct 
impacts on the environment and human societies.

All right, I use 275 words for this essay, and 
I organize it into four paragraphs. I started

my introduction with two introductory 
sentences, and then I paraphrased the

statement. In my first body paragraph, I 
discuss view one, and in the second one,

I discuss view two. And in my conclusion, I stated 
my opinion and summarized all the main points.

In this slide, you can find the points that 
gave cohesion to my writing. You could pause,

take a screenshot, and have a 
read. And in the next three slides,

you will find the words and collocations from 
my essay that you could also use in yours.

All right, I hope you found it 
useful. Have a nice day. [Music]

transcripts.title: IELTS Writing Task 2 Sample Essay (Discuss both views; Give opinion -- Zoos; Cruel or Useful)

segments.text: Welcome to Guide2IELTS
In this video, we'll first plan
and then write an essay on
this topic about zoos
Before we start the planning though, let's get a couple of concepts clear
Zoos are a place or an establishment
where wild animals are kept
usually for display to the public
In zoos, animals are kept in cages or in enclosures
However, zoos are different from wildlife sanctuaries and national parks
where only those animals are kept
which can no longer survive in the wild
and national parks and wildlife sanctuaries are
protected natural habitats
Natural habitat of an organism
a plant or an animal
is an area where they naturally live
So, wildlife sanctuaries and national parks
provide an environment for these animals
which is close to their natural habitat
Let's start with the question now
Some people think that zoos are cruel
and all the zoos should be closed
However, others think that zoos are useful
to protect rare animals
Discuss both views and give your own opinion
As we always do--First step:
read the question and understand it properly
There are two opinions mentioned here
First: zoos are cruel
so, close them
Second: zoos are useful
So, what it does mean is that we shouldn't close them
Another important point is that the question mentions rare animals
Now, the term rare animals refers to animals
which are uncommon
They're different from endangered animals or species
Endangered species are those which are under threat of extinction
Because of human activities such as poaching or deforestation
the numbers of these animals have reduced
Rare animals are under threat too
but because of their small population and
because they are limited to a certain region
So, they are vulnerable because
if there are any disasters in those regions
they could get extinct
Because the question type is
discuss both views and give your own opinion
we need to talk about both sides
and then conclude by mentioning
which side we support
It is also correct to take the middle path
For example,
we could write that
Both sides have valid arguments in their favour
because of which, in my opinion
zoos are important
but we need to ensure that the animals there
are treated humanely
So, in this example conclusion statement
I have conditionally agreed to the second opinion
Bottom line is
your conclusion depends on what points you have made
Another example of conclusion is
if you have 2 points supporting the first opinion
and 4 points for the second one
you could write
Although there are a few reasons to support closing of zoos
the arguments for benefits of zoos are more
Therefore, in my opinion
zoos are helpful to the society
and should remain open
I could just end here
Or in this case, I could add a sentence
However, it should be ensured that
the animals in the zoos are treated humanely
This is why it is crucial that you plan the essay
Planning helps you structure the essay properly
and helps you figure out your conclusion
If your conclusion doesn’t match the points you have made
it will amount to a lower band
The basic writing format or template that we'll follow is
first paragraph: we'll have the introduction
This introduction can just be paraphrasing of the question
that is, writing the question in different words
or it could have some kind of a general statement
followed by mentioning the two opinions that are there in the question
After that, we'll write a statement
that we're going to be discussing both the points in the essay
The next two paragraphs, that is, the second and third paragraphs
are the body paragraphs
In these paragraphs, we discuss
the supporting points for the two opinions that we're given
We start the second paragraph with a topic sentence
that is, a sentence that conveys
which side we'll be talking about, followed by
the supporting points
using beginning words and phrases, such as
firstly or to begin with
followed by secondly, thirdly
or moreover, furthermore
When we start with the third paragraph
we start with a contrasting phrase
such as 'on the other hand'
followed by mentioning that
we'll be talking about the other side now
and then supporting viewpoints of the same
Finally, the fourth paragraph is the conclusion
which gives our opinion
based on the points that we've made in the second and third paragraphs
So, as we always do, when planning this type of question
we make a table, so that there is no confusion
about which side has more points
As I've mentioned before
your conclusion depends on what
points you have made
It doesn't have to be what your actual opinion is
We'll now start planning the essay
First, we'll list out the reasons why
zoos are useful
First point: they are a safe place
for animals, especially from poachers
We can give an example of rare and endangered species here
such as tigers and pandas
The second point
can be about research; they are a good place where scientists can research animals
And the third point could be about edutainment
So, edutainment means education and entertainment together
You can always choose to have two different points of this
one of education, one of entertainment
I've consciously decided not to do so here
because I feel I already have enough points
so I can club these two points together
So, the third point for benefits of zoos include
edutainment. Under edutainment, I can mention
that they're a great place for a family outing
for a school excursion
Also, through zoos we can learn about animals
People, especially children, feel a certain affinity towards them
having seen them
and zoos are also a great place from which we can
spread awareness about environmental issues, etc.
Let's now start planning the other side
regarding cruelty towards animals in zoos
and why they should be closed
One point on this side is that the animals are mistreated there
They're put in confined spaces, they're put in cages
Examples can be given of any animals which
live in forests, which live in open spaces
such as tigers and monkeys
Now, we can write a good essay just with these points and we'll be doing so
However, I'll also give you samples of
better essays which give you more points
What you need to understand is
that you can make a good essay out of very limited points also
If you feel you'll not be able to meet
the required 250 word count
try writing some examples
Just make sure you're using a variety of vocabulary
when writing these points, when writing examples
So, if I write an essay just using this
my essay will be in favour of the viewpoint which says
zoos are useful
I may write in the end that
we should strive to provide animals with bigger spaces
Let's look at a couple of other points that we have
against zoos
One is that when we say
zoos are a great place for entertainment
it is ethically wrong
Hampering the freedom of any living organism for
entertainment, for amusement
cannot be justified morally
Another point:
The animals which are raised in captivity
which are bred in captivity
usually can never be released in the wild
An example of chimpanzees can be given
Chimpanzees which are raised in zoos
which are used for research and for studies
actually cannot survive in the wild
Now, if I write an essay using all these points
I would conclude by saying that zoos need to be closed
There is one more way of handling this essay
We use all these points, we write the conclusion
that zoos should be closed
but then we give other alternatives for
how we can provide education regarding animals
using audio-visual media
how we can have wildlife sanctuaries
national parks for conservation of animals
for studies of animals
Now, this last part is not really asked in the question
So, we'll just include it in the conclusion
Now, before we start the actual writing
between planning and writing
you should just give yourself, maybe a minute or so
to think about what words and synonyms you'd need
when writing on this question
Think of any specific phrases that come to your mind
so as to avoid repetition of words and phrases in the writing
Now, in this case, for a word like 'cruel'
I could write 'mistreatment'
I could just change the word form from 'cruel' to 'cruelty'
For 'closed' I could write 'shut down'
Useful: have benefits
Protect: conserve
Zoos is one word for which we do not have any perfect synonym
We do have words like 'wildlife conservatories'
or 'wildlife parks' but
they're not strictly only for zoos
These words can be used for national parks and wildlife sanctuaries also
So, we'll use these words carefully
Another interesting word that we can use is 'menagerie'
This is a word that is used for
any place where animals
are kept for exhibition
but again, this is not a word which is used only for zoos
Even for other places such as a circus
where animals are there to perform
this word can be used
So, I won't be using these words in the introduction
because that is only about zoos
but later, maybe when I write about zoos being only for entertainment
I could use the word 'menagerie' which would include zoos and other such places
We start with the first paragraph
the introduction
First: a general statement
Visit to a zoo is part of fond childhood memories
of almost everyone
Yet, it has become a contentious subject
in recent years
whether zoos serve an overall positive purpose
There are a few other introductory paragraphs that I've written
They're all on your screen
What you need to notice is that in each of them
I've ensured that my general statement
is connected to the second statement
which is a paraphrasing of the question
Another way to write an introductory paragraph
is to start with a question
Should zoos be encouraged because they serve a useful purpose
or should they be shut down
because animals in zoos are inhumanely
This has become a contentious subject in recent times
which deserves serious thought and discussion
Followed by the introduction
in the first paragraph itself
we can write a statement like:
Since there seem to be valid supporting arguments
for both perspectives
this essay will discuss them
and reach a logical conclusion
based on the presented line of reasoning
Now, we start with the second paragraph
The first sentence would be the topic sentence
which mentions which viewpoint we'll be discussing
in this paragraph
Once again, there are multiple ways in which we could write the topic sentence
A few of them are on your screen
One such topic sentence is
The useful benefits of zoos
have long been known
Now, we start listing out the different points that we have
The first point about zoos being a safe place:
Firstly, these establishments provide a safe place
for wild animals from poachers and other risks
This is especially crucial for rare and endangered species
For example, tigers and pandas are kept in different zoos
across the globe
because of which their numbers have grown manifold
over the years
The second point about them being a location for researchers:
Secondly, these zoological parks are needed
by researchers and scientists to study animals
The third and the last point about this opinion
Lastly, menageries, such as zoos
are ideal location for edutainment
We could just have written
Lastly, zoos are ideal location
for edutainment
But as we've discussed, this is an interesting word that we can incorporate if we know it
Now, we'll go on to list out
how zoos help in edutainment
First, we'll talk about entertainment
and then about the education that we can get from it
Notice the different connectors that we're using
First, we use 'that is to say'
Then, we'll use 'as a result'
That is to say, these establishments
are not only an ideal source of entertainment
for people of all age groups
but they are also a way to impart education
to children regarding animals
As a result of visit to such places
children may feel an affinity towards animals
What is more
zoos are a brilliant means
of creating awareness about environmental issues
Notice that in once place, instead of repeating the word 'zoos'
I've written 'such places'
The third and fourth paragraphs, we'll try to write in different ways
as we've discussed during the planning
If we're choosing to write a lot of points in the third paragraph
or if we're choosing to write a conclusion that is more detailed
by giving alternatives
we can make the second paragraph a little concise
by avoiding the example that we've written
Let's now write the third paragraph
where we write just one point to support the viewpoint
which asks for closure of zoos
We'll start with  a contrasting phrase 'on the other hand'
On the other hand, those who oppose zoos
and want them to be closed
assert that animals are not treated well in the zoos
We use phrases like 'those who oppose this viewpoint'
or 'it is asserted by some people'
when we're trying to convey that this is not my opinion
but opinion of other people
Because in this case, I'm planning to write a strong essayw
which favours the benefits of zoos
I'm using this phrase
Now, we go on to explain
how animals are not treated well
These animals are caged in confined spaces
The cages and enclosures are tiny
compared to their natural habitat
For instance, a tiger which is one of the fastest animals
For instance, a tiger which is one of the fastest animals
Similarly, monkeys which can jump from one tree to another in seconds
are at times confined to a small area
with just 2-3 trees
And we can conclude this essay with
In conclusion, my opinion is that
zoos have many benefits
which have been proven over the years
and they should certainly not be closed
We should, however, ensure that
the animals in zoos have larger spaces
where they can move about
Let's now write the third paragraph in another way
where we write strongly against zoos
On the other hand, it is necessary to accept that
no creature should be treated\
as if it is for entertainment of humankind
Hence, the treatment of wild animals in zoos
has to be considered as brutal
as their freedom is hampered there
What is worse, the animals bred in captivity
are incapable of adapting to the wild environment
and thus can never be released to the wild
For instance, if a chimpanzee is born in a zoo environment
it would never be able to live in a forest
This chimpanzee would not know how to live
with other wild animals or find food for itself
Now, in this case, because I've written stronger points
and more points against than in favour
my conclusion would be:
In conclusion
although it seems that there are quite a few positives of zoos
I strongly believe they are an atrocity
and closing them is the only ethical recourse
We use the word 'recourse'
when we're trying to convey
that this is the only option
to solve a difficult situation
Now, the last possibility that we'd discussed
where we mentioned a few alternatives to zoos
in the conclusion
If I'm planning to do that, in all probabilityJ
I may skip the examples that I've mentioned in the 2nd & 3rd paragraphs
So, in that case, my conclusion will start the same way
as the second sample by saying
In conclusion, although it seems that there are quite a few positives of zoos
I strongly believe they are an atrocity
and closing them is the only ethical recourse
As far as education is concerned
we now have a wide range of audio-visual media
A practical example of this
is the fact that no child has ever seen a dinosaur
but through movies, documentaries, animations, and museums
they are very familiar with these extinct creatures
Similarly, for research and conservation purposes
wildlife sanctuaries and national parks should be encouraged
where the focus is the well-being of the animals
and not the amusement of human visitors
You can find these essays and more
on our website Guide2IELTS.com
What always needs to be kept in mind is
that there is no one single way to answer a question
what I'm trying say here is
that we need to ensure that we answer exactly what is asked
And it is not necessary to have a lot of points to do so
If you found this video useful
do like it, share it with others
comment
and finally , most importantly
subscribe to our channel
All the very best!

towards animals in zoos and why they should be closed One point on this side is that the animals are mistreated there They're put in confined spaces, they're put in cages Examples can be given of any animals which live in forests, which live in open spaces such as tigers and monkeys Now, we can write a good essay just with these points and we'll be doing so However, I'll also give you samples of better essays which give you more points What you need to understand is that you can make a good essay out of very limited points also If you feel you'll not be able to meet the required 250 word count try writing some examples Just make sure you're using a variety of vocabulary when writing these points, when writing examples So, if I write an essay just using this my essay will be in favour of the viewpoint which says zoos are useful I may write in the end that we should strive to provide animals with bigger spaces Let's look at a couple of other points that we have against zoos One is that when we say zoos are a great place for entertainment it is ethically wrong Hampering the freedom of any living organism for entertainment, for amusement cannot be justified morally Another point: The animals which are raised in captivity which are bred in captivity usually can never be released in the wild An example of chimpanzees can be given Chimpanzees which are raised in zoos which are used for research and for studies actually cannot survive in the wild Now, if I write an essay using all these points I would conclude by saying that zoos need to be closed There is one more way of handling this essay We use all these points, we write the conclusion that zoos should be closed but then we give other alternatives for how we can provide education regarding animals using audio-visual media how we can have wildlife sanctuaries national parks for conservation of animals for studies of animals Now, this last part is not really asked in the question So, we'll just include it in the conclusion Now, before we start the actual writing between planning and writing you should just give yourself, maybe a minute or so to think about what words and synonyms you'd need when writing on this question Think of any specific phrases that come to your mind so as to avoid repetition of words and phrases in the writing Now, in this case, for a word like 'cruel' I could write 'mistreatment' I could just change the word form from 'cruel' to 'cruelty' For 'closed' I could write 'shut down' Useful: have benefits Protect: conserve Zoos is one word for which we do not have any perfect synonym We do have words like 'wildlife conservatories' or 'wildlife parks' but they're not strictly only for zoos These words can be used for national parks and wildlife sanctuaries also So, we'll use these words carefully Another interesting word that we can use is 'menagerie' This is a word that is used for any place where animals are kept for exhibition but again, this is not a word which is used only for zoos Even for other places such as a circus where animals are there to perform this word can be used So, I won't be using these words in the introduction because that is only about zoos but later, maybe when I write about zoos being only for entertainment I could use the word 'menagerie' which would include zoos and other such places We start with the first paragraph the introduction First: a general statement Visit to a zoo is part of fond childhood memories of almost everyone Yet, it has become a contentious subject in recent years whether zoos serve an overall positive purpose There are a few other introductory paragraphs that I've written They're all on your screen What you need to notice is that in each of them I've ensured that my general statement is connected to the second statement which is a paraphrasing of the question Another way to write an introductory paragraph is to start with a question Should zoos be encouraged because they serve a useful purpose or should they be shut down because animals in zoos are inhumanely This has become a contentious subject in recent times which deserves serious thought and discussion Followed by the introduction in the first paragraph itself we can write a statement like: Since there seem to be valid supporting arguments for both perspectives this essay will discuss them and reach a logical conclusion based on the presented line of reasoning Now, we start with the second paragraph The first sentence would be the topic sentence which mentions which viewpoint we'll be discussing in this paragraph Once again, there are multiple ways in which we could write the topic sentence A few of them are on your screen One such topic sentence is The useful benefits of zoos have long been known Now, we start listing out the different points that we have The first point about zoos being a safe place: Firstly, these establishments provide a safe place for wild animals from poachers and other risks This is especially crucial for rare and endangered species For example, tigers and pandas are kept in different zoos across the globe because of which their numbers have grown manifold over the years The second point about them being a location for researchers: Secondly, these zoological parks are needed by researchers and scientists to study animals The third and the last point about this opinion Lastly, menageries, such as zoos are ideal location for edutainment We could just have written Lastly, zoos are ideal location for edutainment But as we've discussed, this is an interesting word that we can incorporate if we know it Now, we'll go on to list out how zoos help in edutainment First, we'll talk about entertainment and then about the education that we can get from it Notice the different connectors that we're using First, we use 'that is to say' Then, we'll use 'as a result' That is to say, these establishments are not only an ideal source of entertainment for people of all age groups but they are also a way to impart education to children regarding animals As a result of visit to such places children may feel an affinity towards animals What is more zoos are a brilliant means of creating awareness about environmental issues Notice that in once place, instead of repeating the word 'zoos' I've written 'such places' The third and fourth paragraphs, we'll try to write in different ways as we've discussed during the planning If we're choosing to write a lot of points in the third paragraph or if we're choosing to write a conclusion that is more detailed by giving alternatives we can make the second paragraph a little concise by avoiding the example that we've written Let's now write the third paragraph where we write just one point to support the viewpoint which asks for closure of zoos We'll start with  a contrasting phrase 'on the other hand' On the other hand, those who oppose zoos and want them to be closed assert that animals are not treated well in the zoos We use phrases like 'those who oppose this viewpoint' or 'it is asserted by some people' when we're trying to convey that this is not my opinion but opinion of other people Because in this case, I'm planning to write a strong essayw which favours the benefits of zoos I'm using this phrase Now, we go on to explain how animals are not treated well These animals are caged in confined spaces The cages and enclosures are tiny compared to their natural habitat For instance, a tiger which is one of the fastest animals For instance, a tiger which is one of the fastest animals Similarly, monkeys which can jump from one tree to another in seconds are at times confined to a small area with just 2-3 trees And we can conclude this essay with In conclusion, my opinion is that zoos have many benefits which have been proven over the years and they should certainly not be closed We should, however, ensure that the animals in zoos have larger spaces where they can move about Let's now write the third paragraph in another way where we write strongly against zoos On the other hand, it is necessary to accept that no creature should be treated\ as if it is for entertainment of humankind Hence, the treatment of wild animals in zoos has to be considered as brutal as their freedom is hampered there What is worse, the animals bred in captivity are incapable of adapting to the wild environment and thus can never be released to the wild For instance, if a chimpanzee is born in a zoo environment it would never be able to live in a forest This chimpanzee would not know how to live with other wild animals or find food for itself Now, in this case, because I've written stronger points and more points against than in favour my conclusion would be: In conclusion although it seems that there are quite a few positives of zoos I strongly believe they are an atrocity and closing them is the only ethical recourse We use the word 'recourse' when we're trying to convey that this is the only option to solve a difficult situation Now, the last possibility that we'd discussed where we mentioned a few alternatives to zoos in the conclusion If I'm planning to do that, in all probabilityJ I may skip the examples that I've mentioned in the 2nd & 3rd paragraphs So, in that case, my conclusion will start the same way as the second sample by saying In conclusion, although it seems that there are quite a few positives of zoos I strongly believe they are an atrocity and closing them is the only ethical recourse As far as education is concerned we now have a wide range of audio-visual media A practical example of this is the fact that no child has ever seen a dinosaur but through movies, documentaries, animations, and museums they are very familiar with these extinct creatures Similarly, for research and conservation purposes wildlife sanctuaries and national parks should be encouraged where the focus is the well-being of the animals and not the amusement of human visitors You can find these essays and more on our website Guide2IELTS.com What always needs to be kept in mind is that there is no one single way to answer a question what I'm trying say here is that we need to ensure that we answer exactly what is asked And it is not necessary to have a lot of points to do so If you found this video useful do like it, share it
